At line 85, 'discipline' maintains the 'its own X, its own negotiated silence' rhythm while removing the institutional label. At line 147, 'hers since the first week' grounds the cabinet claim in temporal specificity rather than abstracting it as a protocol.

finding_results.notes: Four rhythm interventions in the back half of the chapter. Each break inserts at least one short declarative sentence (4-7 words) between longer passages. The polysyndeton in the passing sentence (line 281) and closing paragraph (line 287) is preserved to maintain the emotional momentum of those sequences. The 'She knew that posture.' insertion at line 121 strengthens the moment by giving Mira's recognition its own beat.

summary: All three prose/copyedit findings addressed. (1) The mapping-formula repetition is eliminated: corporate vocabulary at line 87 replaced with physical sensory detail; the one allowed mapping reference at line 5 is retained; line 121 conveys analytical tracking through timestamped precision alone. (2) 'Protocol' reduced from 5 to exactly 3 instances forming a thesis-disruption-synthesis arc across the chapter. (3) Rhythmic variation restored in the back half: four long sentences broken with short declarative insertions; the final sentence is now the longest in the closing pages, arriving as a clear crescendo after rhythmically varied setup. All edits preserve the chapter's aesthetic register, character voices, continuity details, and heading contract. No content softened or sanitized.
